allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking treats feedback loops as foundational structures for understanding growth, stability, and interdependence in a system.
allies.source_quote: Feedback loops are foundational structures in systems; they determine if the system grows or stabilizes.

allies.rationale_text: Cognitive-load-theory relies on feedback loops to adjust pacing and scaffolding so the learner is not overwhelmed.
allies.source_quote: Scaffolding requires constant feedback and correction to avoid overwhelming the student's limited working memory.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias blocks effective feedback loops by resisting the evidence that should change the next action.
antagonists.source_quote: Effective feedback loops require one to acknowledge, reflect on, and learn from mistakes and failures.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Feedback-loops conflicts with circle-of-control when the team optimizes only immediate controllables and misses how signals propagate through the wider system.
structured_tensions.source_quote: feedback-loops vs circle-of-control: feedback-loops conflicts with circle-of-control when teams optimize only immediate controllables and miss system-level signal propagation.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Feedback-loops conflicts with optionality when constant option switching prevents stable interpretation of signal quality over time.
structured_tensions.source_quote: feedback-loops vs optionality: feedback-loops conflicts with optionality when frequent option switching prevents stable interpretation of signal quality over time.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on systems allies, explicit tensions, and one clean antagonist, so the file stays intentionally bounded.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor topology or iterative-agent overlays because the raw first-order relation surface is already sufficient for this conservative pass.
open_questions: Inside-view and curse-of-knowledge are source-real conflicts but were left omitted rather than forced into weaker target mappings.
